title: Chintala Venkataramana Temple, Tadipatri
tagline: Chintala Venkataramana Temple, Tadipatri — spiritual town of Andhra Pradesh

heroImage.alt: Chintala Venkataramana Temple, Tadipatri, Andhra Pradesh

overview.short: Chintalarayaswamy Temple or Sri Chintala Venkataramana Temple is a Hindu Vaishnavite temple situated in Tadipatri, a town in the Anantapur District of Andhra Pradesh, India.
overview.description: Chintalarayaswamy Temple or Sri Chintala Venkataramana Temple is a Hindu Vaishnavite temple situated in Tadipatri, a town in the Anantapur District of Andhra Pradesh, India. The Temple is dedicated to Venkateswara, a form of Vishnu, who is referred to as Chintala Venkataramana. The temple was built by Pemmasani Timmanayudu II of the Pemmasani Nayaks. It is situated on the bank of the Penna River, which passes through the town.

topPlaces.name: Bugga Ramalingeswara Swamy Temple
topPlaces.description: Bugga Ramalingeswara Swamy Temple (Telugu: బుగ్గరామలింగేశ్వర స్వామి ఆలయం) is a Shiva shrine situated on the southern bank of the Penna River in Tadipatri, Anantapur district of Andhra Pradesh, India. It was built between 1490 and 1509 by Pemmasani Ramalinga Nayudu I, a chieftain of the Gutti-Gandikota region during the reign of the Vijayanagara Empire.

topPlaces.name: Tadipatri
topPlaces.description: Tadipatri is a town in Anantapur district of the Indian state of Andhra Pradesh at the border of Nandyala district and Kadapa district. It is a Selection Grade Municipality City Council.

items.note: Bugga Ramalingeswara Swamy Temple (Telugu: బుగ్గరామలింగేశ్వర స్వామి ఆలయం) is a Shiva shrine situated on the southern bank of the Penna River in Tadipatri, Anantapur district of Andhra Pradesh, India. It was built between 1490 and 1509 by Pemmasani Ramalinga Nayudu I, a chieftain of the Gutti-Gandikota region during the reign of the Vijayanagara Empire.
Tadipatri is a town in Anantapur district of the Indian state of Andhra Pradesh at the border of Nandyala district and Kadapa district. It is a Selection Grade Municipality City Council.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Chintala Venkataramana Temple, Tadipatri?
faq.a: The best time to visit Chintala Venkataramana Temple, Tadipatri is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Chintala Venkataramana Temple, Tadipatri?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Kurnool Airport (89 km) and the nearest railway station is Tirupati Railway Station (208 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Chintala Venkataramana Temple, Tadipatri?
faq.a: 2 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Chintala Venkataramana Temple, Tadipatri at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Chintala Venkataramana Temple, Tadipatri?
faq.a: Stays in Chintala Venkataramana Temple, Tadipatri start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Chintala Venkataramana Temple, Tadipatri?
faq.a: The top attractions are Bugga Ramalingeswara Swamy Temple, Tadipatri.
